title: MLP Structure Minimalism
research_question: Does the model really need a full dense project-up then project-down MLP in every block, or is that inherited structure now overbuilt on the hybrid frontier?
implementation_notes: Requires new MLP structure modes beyond the current activation-only MLP_MODE surface.
Likely needs support for no-expand tokenwise MLPs, structured linear-plus-quadratic paths, and depth-selective MLP implementations.
Should preserve the current hybrid lower-four-mixer plus shared skip-gate frontier as the base backbone.

candidates.name: y1-no-expand-quadratic-all-layers
candidates.goal: Test the blunt childlike version of the question first.
candidates.hypothesis: A per-token in-place quadratic transform may retain enough MLP value without a full hidden expansion.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether hidden expansion is core or mostly a convenience.
candidates.why_worthy: This attacks one of the most unquestioned structural assumptions in the model.
candidates.description: replace every block MLP with an in-place quadratic token transform and one learned output mixing step

candidates.name: y2-half-width-dense-mlp
candidates.goal: Test whether the MLP still needs expansion, but much less of it.
candidates.hypothesis: The model still wants dense expand-project FFNs, but current expansion width is larger than necessary.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the win is in expansion at all or just in having some nonlinear hidden room.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest control between full structure and total removal of expansion.
candidates.description: cut the MLP hidden expansion in half everywhere while keeping the same relu^2 family

candidates.name: y3-structured-linear-plus-quadratic-no-up
candidates.goal: Test whether feature shaping, not width, is the useful part of the MLP.
candidates.hypothesis: A structured linear-plus-quadratic token path without a full project-up can recover most of the useful MLP behavior.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the live polynomial hint can replace wide hidden expansion.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most justified nontrivial no-expand design because relu-plus-quadratic already showed promise.
candidates.description: replace the dense MLP with a structured linear-plus-quadratic tokenwise path without full hidden expansion

candidates.name: y4-light-lower-full-upper-mlp
candidates.goal: Test whether lower mixer-heavy layers need much less per-token compute.
candidates.hypothesis: The lower stack may no longer need expensive FFNs once it is already mixer-heavy, while upper attention layers still do.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether MLP richness is a depth-specific resource.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly follows the repo's strongest theme: different depths want different jobs.
candidates.description: use a light no-expand MLP only in lower mixer layers and keep the full dense MLP in upper attention layers

candidates.name: y5-full-top-light-rest-mlp
candidates.goal: Test whether rich per-token refinement is mainly a top-of-stack job.
candidates.hypothesis: Only the upper output-facing layers need a full dense MLP, while the rest can use a lighter tokenwise path.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether MLP burden is concentrated near the top rather than spread uniformly.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly challenges the assumption that every layer deserves the same MLP strength.
candidates.description: keep a full dense MLP only near the top of the stack and use a lighter tokenwise MLP elsewhere
